content:
- Completed the no-quorum integrity task end-to-end, including the real fail-safe recovery fix, task-state reconciliation, Ralph task switch, commit, and push.
- Final verification was green: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint all passed after the code and contract-test updates.
- Current HEAD is pushed (commit ba9835a). No AGENTS.md updates were necessary because the learning was task-specific and already captured in the task markdown.

- Read the progress log (no prior content surfaced) and loaded .ralph/current_tasks.md plus .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Active backlog currently shows only failing tasks plus one recurring meta-task; all listed bug tasks in done registry currently pass true.
- Next I need to inspect active task files for blocked_by and priority tags, then pick the highest-precedence unblocked task and write its path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Checked active task files directly: no active task has blocked_by tags, no active failing bugs remain, and there is no active ultra_high chain that would force a different story.
- Sanity-checked repo shape: the codebase still has broad centralized logging/event usage across src/logging, src/process, and operator docs, which makes the typed-event foundation task the strongest next failing task.
- Selecting .ralph/tasks/story-tracing-based-logging/01-task-establish-typed-event-contract-and-emit-ownership-rules.md and writing it to .ralph/current_task.txt now, then stopping immediately.

- Started the new story-tracing-based-logging task by reading the prior Codex progress entry, the current Ralph task pointer, and the active task markdown.
- Confirmed this turn is in planning mode because the task file still has no explicit plan / TO BE VERIFIED / NOW EXECUTE marker.
- Gathered current repo context from src/logging/mod.rs and the sibling story task files. Found one stale reference already: the story text mentions src/api/events.rs and ingest_wal_event, but that file does not exist in the current tree, so the plan must explicitly correct the inventory and downstream task scopes.
- Next: write a detailed typed-event-first implementation plan into the active task file, end it with TO BE VERIFIED, then stop immediately.

- Wrote the detailed implementation plan into .ralph/tasks/story-tracing-based-logging/01-task-establish-typed-event-contract-and-emit-ownership-rules.md.
- The plan now includes the execution order, typed logging contract shape, raw-record builder work, test-helper work, story rewrite requirements for Tasks 02-04, and the concrete stale-reference correction for src/api/events.rs.
- Added the required TO BE VERIFIED marker and stopped before any implementation work, matching the Ralph workflow for this phase.
- Next time: read the task file, perform a deep skeptical review of the plan, change at least one thing, replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE only once the revised plan is solid.

- Read the Ralph progress history and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Confirmed the active task path is .ralph/tasks/story-tracing-based-logging/01-task-establish-typed-event-contract-and-emit-ownership-rules.md.
- Next I am reading the task file to determine whether this turn is the skeptical plan review phase or execution phase.

- Read the active task markdown and found it in TO BE VERIFIED state, so this turn is the skeptical review phase.
- Audited the repository emit inventory with ripgrep and confirmed the story still contains stale references, especially src/api/events.rs which does not exist.
- Found a second plan problem: it still tells the future executor to re-audit during execution, which conflicts with the Ralph rule that NOW EXECUTE should avoid new exploration. I am tightening the plan with exact corrections and explicit execution steps before promoting it.

- Completed the skeptical review of Task 01 and changed the plan before promoting it.
- Removed the stale src/api/events.rs reference from the Task 01 inventory, replaced the inaccurate emit-count summary with the audited inventory, and tightened the plan so NOW EXECUTE does not depend on another discovery pass.
- Also changed the plan language around EventMeta/emit_event so no new compatibility shim is introduced; any temporary adapter must stay thin and be deleted before the story is complete.
- Set the task marker to NOW EXECUTE and stopped without implementation work so the next turn can execute the revised plan directly.

- Read the active task markdown and confirmed it is in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Execution starts with the plan order: update the story markdown files first, then implement the typed logging contract in src/logging, then run the full required verification suite.
- Next I am reading the targeted logging and story files needed for direct execution without reopening discovery.

- Patched Tasks 01-04 first, per the execution order.
- Removed the stale src/api/events.rs references from the story set and tightened Tasks 03-04 so they explicitly depend on the Task 01 typed event and raw-record contract.
- Next I am implementing the logging contract in src/logging with a thin legacy adapter, plus shared raw-record builders and test helpers.

- Implemented the Task 01 logging contract in src/logging/event.rs and src/logging/raw_record.rs, routed LogHandle::emit_event through the typed app-event encoder, and moved runtime/process/postgres-ingest raw line assembly onto shared builders.
- Updated the story task markdown set, added logging-level typed contract tests, and verified the required gates: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint all passed. The first make test-long run had one flaky ultra-long HA failure, but the isolated rerun and the full rerun both passed cleanly.
- Next I am doing the wrap-up steps required by the task protocol: task markdown status already marked complete with passes=true, then task_switch.sh, commit, and push.
